commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `tools.fs.workspaceAliases` to allow configured relative workspace paths to resolve to exact host targets for workspace-only read/write/edit and memory-flush writes.

PR surface: Source +316, Tests +170, Docs +1. Total +487 across 13 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main lacks `workspaceAliases`, current tests reject outside-workspace symlink parents under `workspaceOnly`, and memory-flush runs force workspace-only policy. I did not run a live reproduction because this pass was read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blockers are maintainer security/product judgment and contributor real-behavior proof, not a narrow code repair ClawSweeper should automate.

target.title: feat(fs): allow configured workspace aliases
target.body:
Summary
- Add `tools.fs.workspaceAliases` for exact in-workspace aliases when `tools.fs.workspaceOnly` is enabled.
- Allow read/write/edit and memory-flush append-only writes through a configured alias whose realpath exactly matches the configured target.
- Keep direct target paths outside scope, preserve default symlink escape rejection, and document the new config surface.

Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.workspace-paths.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.workspace-only-false.test.ts src/agents/tool-fs-policy.test.ts src/config/config.schema-regressions.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: `openclaw.write`/memory flush can append to `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md` when `memory` is a configured workspace alias symlinked outside the agent workspace, without disabling `tools.fs.workspaceOnly`.
Real environment tested: Local Linux checkout based on `origin/main` at `1af55bc665`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.workspace-paths.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.workspace-only-false.test.ts src/agents/tool-fs-policy.test.ts src/config/config.schema-regressions.test.ts`; `git diff --check`.
Evidence after fix: `agent-tools.workspace-paths.test.ts` covers a `memory` symlink that resolves outside the workspace, verifies read/write/edit through the configured alias, verifies direct target writes still reject, and verifies memory-triggered append-only writes through the alias.
Observed result after fix: Vitest passed 3 shards: 4 test files, 79 tests; `git diff --check` produced no output.
What was not tested: Full `pnpm check`/broad CI and a live deployed OpenClaw memory flush run.

Closes #90977
target.author: stevenepalmer
